series_description:
Original notices of: duty appointments,
removal from appointment, certification
upgrades, certification applications, testing
applications, renewal invoices, and applications
for International Fire Service Accreditation
Congress (IFSAC) seals.
comments:
The paper forms submitted to the
agency will be held for the retention
period listed. The data provided by the
documents will be retained for the time
period listed under Agency Item
Number M-003 or M-004.

series_description:
Documentation of certificate holders'
emergency medical training records, college
transcripts, course completions & testing
records.
comments:
Documents submitted to the agency
will be held for the retention period
listed. The data provided by the
documents will be retained for the time
period listed under Agency Item
Number M-003.

series_description:
Individual records entered or submitted into
the agency web application for storage in
agency databases.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years

series_description:
Entity records entered or submitted into the
agency web application for storage in agency
databases. Entities include: fire departments
currently or formerly regulated by the agency;
law enforcement entities that hold arson
commissions.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description: Initial training facility application.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Curriculum developed for the training of
disciplines certified by the agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Performance testing skills required for
certification in a discipline.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Form used to match state examination
questions to requisite knowledge objectives
and performance testing skills to skills
objectives listed in the NFPA standards for a
specific discipline.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Course information, completions and course
exam grades entered into agency web
applications for storage in agency databases.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Requests for academy class exams, exam
rosters, and academy invoices.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Agency application containing all state
examination questions for each discipline, as
well as statistics and reference notes related to
each question.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
Previously used questions are made
inactive but are not deleted from the
question bank.

series_description:
Selected questions for individually
administered state examination.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description: Final grades from state examination.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Examinee's answer sheet for first failed state
examination.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Feedback on state examination questions and
conditions from individual examinees, in either
written or online formats.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Written requests for special testing
accommodations.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
Agency-maintained list of approved courses
used by individuals to qualify for higher levels
of certification.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments: Relevance can span 20-30 years.

series_description:
Documents submitted for a training
equivalency review (Records Review Request).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Borrowing lists for audiovisual materials and
books.

Information submitted to the agency regarding
details of fire fighter injuries.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Investigation case files maintained on agency
databases, which are logged via the agency's
web application.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Includes compliance and injury
investigations.

series_description:
Documents or evidence (on paper or in
electronic format) collected during an
investigation, including ancillary
documentation maintained in agency file
storage.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
Includes compliance and injury
investigations. Includes acceptable
Plans of Corrective Action.

series_title:
Training Facility and Fire
Department Inspection Files
series_description:
Inspections conducted on training facilities
and/or fire departments either biannually or
on an at-risk basis.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
IFSAC accreditation paperwork, including
reaccreditation application, self-study
documents, and IFSAC site team report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
A plan outlining agency goals on energy and
water consumption.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Settlement agreement between agency staff
and regulated entities/ individuals re: penalties
for administrative violations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Data saved in agency databases which
duplicates existing records, and is determined
to have no value.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Care must be taken that the
record is a true duplicate.

series_description:
Data stored in a temporary table within a
database.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Audits and reviews of training facilities
performed by or on behalf of the agency,
including the working papers that support the
audit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Training facility audits performed on
behalf of the agency.

series_description:
Audits and reviews of an individual certificate
holder's continuing education performed by or
on behalf of the agency, including the working
papers that support the audit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
Continuing education audits performed
on behalf of the agency.

series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations requests by an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Legislative Appropriation Requests
must be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Any document that officially initiates, rescinds,
or amends general office procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure that
governs the programs, services, or projects of
an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or programs
and scheduling, or itinerary records maintained
by staff during business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other activities
of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars,
appointment, and itinerary records of
elected officials, agency heads, and
board or commission members, require
archival review. Contact the State
Archives when these records have met
their retention periods. CAUTION: A
record of this type purchased with
personal funds but used by a state
official or employee to document his or
her work activities may be a state
record and subject to this retention
period. See Open Records Decision 635
issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency. Includes
print, electronic, audio, and audiovisual
records.

Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for records
that are furnished to the public under Public
Information Act (Chapter 552, Government
Code). Also includes withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for records
that are excepted under the Public Information
Act (Chapter 552, Government Code), including
records provided to the legislature under
section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure of
an agency and the relationships and relative
ranks of its departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts showing
the overall arrangement and
administrative structure of the state
agency need to be transferred to the
Archives and Information Services
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission. Organizational
charts showing division/department
level detail are not considered archival.

series_description:
Plans and records relating to the process of
planning new or redefined programs, services,
or projects of an agency that are neither
included in nor directly related to other records
series in this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Data processing
planning records are not archival.

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn, emergency,
and adopted rules; open meetings notices; or
any other documents required by law to be
submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Drafts of proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by or
on behalf of executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors and program heads require
archival review.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an agency in
anticipation of or in the adjudication of a
lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set legal
precedent or exhibit historical value
will be evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission for archival preservation.

series_description:
Logs or similar records used to register persons
appearing before state agencies as required by
Chapter 2004, Government Code, including
quarterly reports filed with the Texas Ethics
Commission.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002.
Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating the final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
Strategic Plans must be submitted to
the Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(c).
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival
review and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Self evaluations and plans documenting
compliance with the requirements of the
Americans With Disabilities Act.

Records of temporary usefulness that are not
an integral part of a records series of an
agency, that are not regularly filed within an
agency’s recordkeeping system, and that are
required only for a limited period of time for
the completion of an action by an official or
employee of the agency or in the preparation
of an ongoing records series.
Transitory records are not essential to the
fulfillment of statutory obligations or to the
documentation of agency functions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers should make certain records
are not part of another records series
listed in this schedule or, for records
series unique to an agency, are not part
of a records series that documents the
fulfillment of the statutory obligations
of the agency or the documentation of
its functions. The disposal of transitory
information need not be documented
through destruction signoffs (1.2.001)
or in records disposition logs (1.2.010),
but agencies should establish
procedures governing disposal of these
records as part of its records
management plan (1.2.014).

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes of Open
series_description:
Agendas and minutes of state boards,
committees, commissions, and councils that
conduct open meetings as required by Chapter
551, Government Code.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Agency retains
permanent record copy. The archival
requirement (see Texas Government
Code, Section 324.008(d)) will be met
by sending a copy to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission.
CAUTION: This records series and item
numbers 1.1.059, 1.1.060, 1.1.061, and
1.1.062 must be used for those state
boards, committees, commissions, and
councils, which by law or the biennial
Appropriations Act, are administered
by another state agency. These records
and all others related to the functions
of any of these dependent entities
must be included in the records
retention schedule of the administering
agency.

series_description:
Minutes or notes, and supporting
documentation, taken at internal agency staff
meetings.

Biennial narrative reports to the governor and
legislature as required by an agency's enabling
statutes, including annual narrative reports if
they are required by statute. Includes working
files and related documentation used in
creating final report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of
these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3. The requirement is met by
sending the required copies of the
reports to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program, Texas State
Library and Archives Commission.
Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final report may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules, or
similar records distributed internally for the
use of employees or externally to the public or
those individuals or entities regulated by an
agency that sets out the rules, policies, and
procedures that govern an agency's programs,
services, or projects. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final rules, policies, and procedures,
are not subject to archival requirement
and may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of the Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per Government
Code 552.010.

Transcripts and final decisions of hearings
conducted as part of the regulatory process,
and hearings on proposed rules and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that lead
to substantial or statutory changes to
the operations of the agency and its
policies and procedures should be
retained for archival review. Contact
the State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related information,
including meeting notices, proofs of
publication, and meeting minutes, and
require a longer retention period.

series_title:
Sunset Review Report and
Related Documentation
series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study, and
other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset review
process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final recommendations.
The final version of the agency self-
evaluation report must be submitted to
the Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).

series_description:
Final agreement described by Government
Code 2009.054(c), associated with a matter
conducted under an alternative dispute
resolution procedure in which personnel of a
state agency participated as a party on the
agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice and
Remedies Code,
Chapter 154.071.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used internally and
externally by the agency including subsequent
revisions to an agency form or any associated
design or design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e. form SLR
105) that identifies the records that are
created and maintained by an agency, provides
the minimum timeframes the records must be
retained, and includes instructions for their
disposition. This series may include working
files and documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by the
State and Local Records Management
Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Forms used to track the transmittal of records
to/from onsite and offsite storage areas or a
transfer of physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records disposed
of by an agency, which might include records
series title, dates of records, and date of
disposition.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to capture
records inventory information, including
location tracking and to document existence
and requirements of a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Documentation used to provide and improve
access to records. Includes indexes, card files,
shelf lists, registers, guides, and other finding
aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and archival
code of the records they support.

series_description:
Records management plans and similar records
that establish the policies and procedures
under which records and information are
managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Transmittals and related service
documentation (e.g. form RMD 109) for
disaster recovery services provided by TSLAC or
other entities.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Forms used by TSLAC (e.g. form RMD 113) or
other entities to establish disaster recovery
services, authorize agency staff to access the
media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
One copy of each state publication as defined
on page xi of this schedule, except a
publication that is subject to a different
retention period in this schedule. For example,
a meeting agenda (see item number 1.1.058)
also meets the definition, but it must be
retained permanently; item numbers 1.1.004,
1.1.055, 1.1.066, 1.1.068, and 4.5.003, which
also meet the definition, are closely associated
with the appropriations process and must be
retained AC + 6.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program, Texas
State Library and Archives Commission,
by law (Government Code, 441.103-
441.105). The Texas State Library and
Archives Commission will retain a copy
of all state publications submitted to it
on a continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if the
publication merits further retention.
For additional information and
requirements concerning state
publications made available to the
public through the Internet, consult the
administrative rules of the Texas State
Publications Depository Program of the
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission (13 TAC §§3.1-3.8).

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files containing
organized and consistent sets of complete and
accurate electronic records and associated
processing files. Examples include, but are not
limited to, data tables of relational databases
used by applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC to
agency master files and application
data that the files do not fall under
records series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files and
associated processing files including job control
language, programs, applications, scripts,
source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for access
to electronic records must be retained
for the period of time required to
access the records.

series_description:
Records documenting operational and
maintenance requirements of computer
hardware such as operating manuals,
hardware/operating system requirements,
hardware configurations, equipment control
systems, and associated processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for access
to electronic records must be retained
for the period of time required to
access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to document
modifications to computer programs; to access,
retrieve, manipulate, and interpret data in an
automated system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include but are
not limited to: user guides, system or sub-
system definitions, system specifications, input
and output specifications, system flow charts,
program flow charts, program maintenance
logs, change notices, data element dictionaries,
file layouts, code books or tables, and other
records that explain the meaning, purpose,
structure, logical relationships, and origin of
the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic records
must be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits. Records
include but are not limited to: reports showing
transactions accepted, rejected, suspended,
and/or processed; history files or tapes;
records of updates to application files; and
security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Chargeback Records to Data
Processing Services Users
series_description:
Records documenting usage, costs, billing, cost
recovery, budgeting, and administrative
functions of computer usage and data
processing services for individual
units/departments/ divisions in an agency.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Forms and logs used to enter and reconcile
data sets submitted for processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to activate
the software for legal use by the end users of a
state agency, along with warranties providing
that the software will perform in accordance
with functional specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency's
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters of
reference, and similar documents whose
submission by candidates for vacant positions
is required on the application form, by
application procedures, or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to the
counseling of an employee for work-related,
personal, or substance abuse problems.
Usually maintained at the supervisorial level
except in those agencies with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings, promotions,
training programs, or opportunities for
overtime.

Includes a contract or agreement regarding the
terms and conditions of employment of an
individual and a contract with a vendor for
temporary staffing services. Executed,
renewed, or amended on or after September 1,
2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement regarding the
terms and conditions of employment of an
individual and a contract with a vendor for
temporary staffing services.Executed, renewed,
or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals, or
other similar documents used to evaluate the
performance of an employee.

Disciplinary actions are those actions which
may affect pay, status, or tenure. They are
imposed to discipline an employee whose
conduct is harmful to the best interests of the
state, the agency, or the employee work force;
and for failure to improve performance or
conduct following imposition of corrective
action. May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons for
failure to give written notice of resignation.
May also be used to document evidence of
employee self-improvement efforts, as well as
favorable and unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the records of
an employee including pay grade, position
classification, employee number, evaluation
date, and termination of employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated task
or skill statements, for positions in an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained from
the Department of Public Safety (DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is
authorized to obtain a criminal history
record information from DPS must
refer to its agency's legislation or see
Subchapter F, Chapter 411,
Government Code for appropriate
retention and use of this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing education
achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Summary of applicant qualifications, evaluation
basis for selection or rejection, original
applications, job assignments, promotions,
separations, layoffs, terminations,
compensation records, conditions of work,
time records, hours of training provided
(separate from labor), minority status, and
gender of all selected and rejected applicants.
Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing and
Screening Records - Positive
Results and Calibration
series_description:
Records of employee alcohol test results
indicating an alcohol concentration of 0.02 or
greater; records of employee verified positive
controlled substances test results;
documentation of refusals to take required
alcohol and/or controlled substances tests;
employee evaluation and referrals; calibration
documentation; records related to the
administration of the alcohol and controlled
substances testing programs; copy of each
annual calendar year summary.
legal_citation:
49 CFR 382.403 for
commercial motor
vehicle drivers.

series_title:
Employee Drug Testing and
Screening Records - Negative
Results
series_description:
Records of negative and canceled controlled
substances test results and alcohol test results
with a concentration of less than 0.02.

Agreements between employee and agency
authorizing certain actions, including
acknowledgement forms, telecommuting
agreements, outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation that
show proof of receipt and awareness of and
adherence to agency policies and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for reasonable
accommodation under the ADA (Americans
with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or stop
all voluntary or required deductions from
payroll, including garnishment or other court-
ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Payroll records and registers documenting
employee earnings, wages, and pay. This may
include but is not limited to payroll input
records, summary statements, payroll
vouchers, payroll detail sheets, and payroll
history.
Includes 1099, W2, FICA, and other tax records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or in
the computation of taxes.

Employer's copy of "Employee’s Withholding
Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Records defining the wage or salary rate for
each position in the agency expressed in
dollars, grades, or step numbers.

Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims, including
reimbursement of funds disbursed by Texas
Workforce Commission (TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s earnings
into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Records documenting the amount of pension
or deferred compensation earned by individual
employees.

Reports providing statistical information on
labor force.

Records relating to review and monitoring of
job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew, or case schedules,
rosters, or assignments.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

series_title:
Reimbursable Activities,
Requests and Authorizations
to Engage in
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
work-related activities for which the expenses
of the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Any internally distributed manuals, guidelines,
or similar records that define agency wide
policies and procedures concerning the
personnel of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any documents detailing the procedural duties
and responsibilities of agency positions on a
position-by-position basis.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on aspects
of personnel staffing, including listings of all
staff by program or name, staff hired during a
month, detailed listings of employees within its
organizational structure, position vacancies,
analyses of turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion or
transfer, including validation records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: One copy of each different
master test (different in terms of either
questions or administration
procedures) should be retained for the
period indicated.

series_description:
Completed aptitude, competency, or skills test
papers and checklists of job applicants or of
current personnel taking a test to qualify for
promotion or transfer.

Includes documentation used to complete EEO
(Equal Employment Opportunity) reports.

Diversity and recruitment plans and related
workforce analyses.

Used to create employee leave balances, to
transfer leave balances when an employee
transfers positions, to adjust carry-over
balances at end of fiscal year, to correct errors
on leave accumulation, and to close out leave
accounts on separated employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Cumulative report is issued each pay cycle and
provides employee leave status information for
each position.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting individual employee’s
hours worked, including work schedules and
documentation evidencing adherence to or
deviation from normal hours for those
employees working on fixed schedules.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical Leave
Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and other types of
authorized leave, and supporting
documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Requests submitted, approvals, number of
hours transferred in and out of comprehensive
sick leave pool.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of checks
and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure, and
special vouchers; and similar records that serve
to document disbursements, including those
documenting claims for and reimbursement to
employees for travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants, cash
receipts, credit card receipts, receipts books,
cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that serve
to document money owed to or received by a
state agency and its collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting
System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Yearly report compiled from information
entered into the Uniform Statewide Accounting
System (USAS).
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Sufficient records to verify information on
unclaimed property previously reported to the
State Comptroller showing the name and last
known address of the apparent owner of
reportable unclaimed property, a brief
description of the property, and the balance of
each unclaimed account, if appropriate.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and external
procedural requirements with respect to the
accounting department of a state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Records of accounts deemed uncollectible,
including write-off authorizations and returned
checks, warrants, and drafts.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Documentation relating to the capital and fixed
assets of a state agency, including equipment
or property history cards containing data on
initial cost, depreciation schedules or
summaries used for capital outlay budgeting,
and property sale, auction, or disposal records
of agency owned equipment and property.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting requests for payment of
a sum of money according to the terms of a
policy or contract.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This series documents state, federal, and other
sponsored grant projects participated in or
administered by state agencies. It includes
grant authorization records, which provide
evidence of the award of grants to or by
agencies; grant/project financial or
performance reports, which are periodic
reports of financial activity and/or program
performance related to grants received or
made by agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention requirements
may vary depending on the specific
funding agency. Records in this series
may be subject to Government Code
441.1855. Agencies must ensure that
records are retained for the
appropriate retention period.

series_description:
Non-awarded, denied, or unfunded grant
applications and proposals.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Records documenting financial obligations of a
state agency that are not payable within one
year of the date of the balance sheet, including
debentures, loans, deferred tax liabilities,
bonds, and pension obligations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Signature cards or similar records establishing
authority of an agency employee to initiate or
authorize financial transactions on behalf of an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Logs documenting requests and receipt of FTI,
including any information created by the
recipient that is derived from federal return or
return information received from the IRS or
obtained through a secondary source.

Indirect cost plan and supporting
documentation created or maintained in the
development of the plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Bank statements, credit card statements,
cancelled checks, check registers, deposit slips,
debit and credit notices, reconciliations, and
other banking related records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Required by the General Appropriations Act.
Includes encumbrances and documentation
about budget transfers and revisions, as well as
detail charts of accounts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting incoming or outgoing
deliveries, including through private courier
services.

Any mailing address, telephone or fax number,
or email address records maintained by an
agency on its employees or on entities or
persons it serves.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records and reports of postage expenses,
including postal meter usage.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_title:
Requisitions for In-Agency or
Inter-Agency Copy/Printing
Service
series_description: Includes word processing and data processing.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_title:
Licenses and Permits for Non-
Vehicles
series_description:
Licenses and permits obtained from external
agencies or organizations in order to perform
operations.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include licenses
and permits issued by an agency as part
of its statutory responsibilities.

series_title:
Photocopier and Telefax
Usage Logs & Reports
series_description:
Registers or logs of print copies and fax
transmissions made by user or in total.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Schedules of prices charged by an agency for
services to the public or other agencies,
including any documentation used to
determine the charges.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile,
and other policies for government property
and personnel including supporting
documentation relevant to the
implementation, modification, renewal, or
replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or
amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Liability, theft, fire, health, life, automobile,
and other policies for government property
and personnel including supporting
documentation relevant to the
implementation, modification, renewal, or
replacement of policies. Executed, renewed, or
amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Any internally distributed manual, guidelines,
or similar records that establish standard office
procedures for an agency; e.g. agency style
manuals, telephone protocols, mail room
procedures, print shop and photocopy ordering
instructions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Any record created by an agency to track any
type of incoming and outgoing correspondence
or packages by the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) or
by private couriers.

List of agency contracts, leases, and
agreements including general obligation, land
lease, utilities, and construction contracts.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Assessments or evaluations of the value of
state-owned buildings or property.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Records documenting building space requests
and approvals. Information on request forms
may include but is not limited to: the building
and room; the reason for the change; parties
involved in the request; date of request and
approval.

Records documenting the determination,
checking, or rectifying of any instrument giving
quantitative measurements.
comments:
CAUTION: Some equipment and
instruments may require longer
retention period. State agencies must
determine if longer retention period is
required based on the type of
equipment or instruments they use
within their agency.

retention_statement: Life of Asset
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Estimate Files (Supply and
Repair Cost Estimates)
series_description:
Quotes for minor construction and repair
projects and supplies. Includes supporting
documentation, as applicable.

Requests or work orders for repairs or
maintenance to facilities, vehicles, or
equipment completed by internal agency
personnel or external service providers.

Any type of usage report or log used to
monitor utilities such as gas, electric, water,
etc.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include utility
usage reports for agencies that operate
their own utilities.

series_description:
Equipment and material descriptions and
specifications that may include but are not
limited to detailed descriptions; lists of raw
materials and ingredients; physical
characteristics of items; special handling
procedures; or technical drawings.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Reservation logs or similar records relating to
the use of agency facilities such as meeting
rooms, auditoriums, etc.

Reports summarizing efficiency of facility space
utilization, which may include data on room
usage, demand, allocation, and capacity
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Documentation of mail returned by the postal
or other mail delivery services as undeliverable.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: If corrected address
provided and mail rerouted, maintain
address update documentation for
prescribed retention period.

series_description:
Freight or cargo claims against carrier for
damage or loss to a shipment.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Shipping information, including order
acknowledgements, packing slips, and related
documentation.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
CAUTION: Some shipping information
may require longer retention period.
State agencies must determine if longer
retention period is required based on
the type of materials shipped to or
from their agency.

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications,
awarded and unawarded bids, and bid
tabulation/evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed, renewed,
or amended on or after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes bid requisition/authorizations,
invitations to bid or propose, bid specifications,
awarded and unawarded bids, and bid
tabulation/evaluations.
Associated with a contract executed, renewed,
or amended on or before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
Invalid bids that do not meet agency
submission requirements and are not included
in bid evaluation process (e.g. withdrawn,
missed submission deadline, incomplete
submission, etc.).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Log, register, etc., providing a record of
purchase orders issued, orders received, and
similar data on procurement status.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
W-9 IRS Form used to request a taxpayer
identification number (TIN) for reporting
information to the Internal Revenue Service
(IRS). This includes W-9 forms received by a
state agency from vendors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Plans for evacuation of agency facilities in cases
of emergency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Plan that provides information to employees
about the hazardous chemicals to which they
may be exposed to in their workplace.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 5 years

series_description:
List of each hazardous chemical normally
present in the workplace.

Logs, registers, or similar records documenting
visitors to limited access or restricted areas of
agency facilities.

Records relating to the issuance of keys,
identification cards, building passes,
passwords, signed statements or similar
instruments of access to agency or state
facilities, equipment or automated systems.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: Vital record.

series_description:
Reports of accidents to adults on state
property or in any other situation in which the
state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Reports of accidents to minors on state
property or in any other situation in which the
state agency could be a party to a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
Environmental, biological, and material safety
monitoring reports, including health or physical
examination reports or certificates of
employees, who have experienced exposure to
toxic substances, harmful physical agent, or
bloodborne pathogens in the workplace,
including analyses derived from such reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 30 years

series_description:
Environmental, biological, and material safety
monitoring reports, including health or physical
examination reports or certificates of
employees, for whom periodic monitoring of
health or fitness is required concerning toxic
substances, harmful physical agent, or
bloodborne pathogens in the workplace,
including analyses derived from such reports.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 2 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Vehicle titles, registration information, and
owner manuals for state vehicles.
retention_statement: Life of Asset
